 ---
title: "Curriculum Overview"
---

A structured path through the technical foundations of vision-language models, 
designed for someone coming from physics and applied mathematics.

## Dependency structure

Each stage builds on the previous. The through-line is this: all of these models 
are learning to compress high-dimensional data into lower-dimensional representations. 
What gets preserved and what gets discarded in that compression is the technical 
core of most interesting questions in this space.

## Stages

### Stage 1 -- How neural networks learn
**Core reading:** Nielsen, *Neural Networks and Deep Learning*, chapters 1-2.
[Free online](http://neuralnetworksanddeeplearning.com)

The foundation. Forward pass, loss functions, gradient descent, backpropagation. 
From a physics perspective: optimization in a high-dimensional energy landscape.

### Stage 2 -- Convolutional Neural Networks
**Core reading:** CS231n notes on CNNs (Stanford, free online)

Spatial locality and translation equivariance as inductive biases. 
Convolution as learned feature detection. Residual connections.

### Stage 3 -- Attention and Transformers
**Core reading:** Vaswani et al. 2017 (*Attention is All You Need*) + 
Jay Alammar's *The Illustrated Transformer*

The architecture that replaced everything else. Self-attention as a soft, 
differentiable, input-dependent weighted average. Vision Transformers (ViTs) 
as the direct precursor to CLIP's image encoder.

### Stage 4 -- CLIP and vision-language models
**Core reading:** Radford et al. 2021 (*Learning Transferable Visual Models 
From Natural Language Supervision*)

Contrastive learning, shared embedding spaces, zero-shot classification. 
Why the training data matters enormously.

### Stage 5 -- Evaluation and benchmarks
**Core reading:** Barocas, Hardt, Narayanan -- *Fairness and Machine Learning*, 
selected chapters. [Free online](https://fairmlbook.org)

What benchmarks actually measure. Subgroup performance. The incompatibility 
of fairness metrics.

## Status

| Stage | Status |
|---|---|
| 1 -- Neural networks | in progress |
| 2 -- CNNs | not started |
| 3 -- Attention | not started |
| 4 -- CLIP | not started |
| 5 -- Evaluation | not started |
